title: Malty Midnight Vietnamese Pork Sandwich
description: A malty, shaggy, midnight-craving Vietnamese dish featuring rye bread and pork, perfect for a late night snack. This flexitarian-friendly recipe is made with a tava-griddle.
instructions: Heat a tava-griddle over medium heat and brush with oil.
Add the sliced pork shoulder and cook until browned, about 5 minutes per side.
In a small bowl, whisk together the malt syrup, fish sauce, and lime juice.
Brush the malt syrup mixture onto the pork during the last minute of cooking.
Toast the rye bread slices on the tava-griddle.
Spread mayonnaise on each toasted bread slice.
Assemble the sandwiches by placing the cooked pork onto the bread, then topping with grilled onions, pickled carrots, and cilantro.
Emulsify the mayonnaise and malt syrup mixture by whisking constantly.
Adjust the amount of fish sauce and lime juice to taste.
